varieties.name: 'Ruth Wilcox' (Albo-Lacinatus)
varieties.origin: Australian cultivation name; traditional cultivar, established pre-1950s
varieties.traits: Medium shrub, 1.5-2m tall; pink flowers with white lacinate (deeply fringed) petals; distinctive cut-petal appearance
varieties.availability: Established in Queensland gardens; available through cuttings and specialist nurseries

varieties.name: 'Cooperi' (Variegated Hibiscus)
varieties.origin: 1840s, Polynesian cultivar, early introduction to Australia
varieties.traits: Variegated foliage with white, pink, and green leaves; red flowers; grows 1.5-2m
varieties.availability: Available from Queensland nurseries and Hibiscus specialists

varieties.name: 'Dainty White'
varieties.origin: Mutation from 'Dainty Pink', mid-20th century USA
varieties.traits: Compact shrub, 1-1.5m; pure white single flowers; darker green foliage

varieties.name: 'The Queenslander'
varieties.origin: Australian bred, modern cultivar developed for Queensland conditions
varieties.traits: Maroon/burgundy petals celebrating Queensland sporting colors; creamy stamen core; robust growth
varieties.availability: AW 1770 Botanic Nursery, Caboolture QLD specialist growers

varieties.name: 'Great Idea'
varieties.traits: Spectacular double blooms; vigorous grower; compact to medium shrub 1.5-2m

varieties.name: 'Gwen Mary'
varieties.traits: Ruffled cotton-candy pink petals; delicate red stamen; medium shrub

varieties.name: 'Lady Flo'
varieties.origin: Queensland, named after Senator Lady Flo Bjelke-Petersen
varieties.traits: Elegant soft pink flowers with deeper pink center; medium shrub; Queensland heritage

varieties.name: 'Laurie Hughes'
varieties.traits: Stunning large blooms in rich burgundy with delicate pale edges; striking contrast

varieties.name: 'Lemon Empress'
varieties.traits: Royal bright lemon-yellow petals with contrasting crimson-red center; yellow/orange stamen

varieties.name: 'President's Choice'
varieties.traits: Large ruffled petals with marbled blend of pink, orange, and white; prominent yellow stamen

varieties.name: 'Red Rascal'
varieties.traits: Velvety deep crimson petals with dark center; contrasting white/pink stamens; compact

varieties.name: Hollywood Hibiscus® 'Hot Shot'
varieties.traits: Large vivid red flowers; blooms last 3 days (vs typical 1 day); especially heat tolerant

varieties.name: Hollywood Hibiscus® 'Talk of the Town'
varieties.traits: Blend of medium and light pinks; brilliant magenta center; very heat tolerant

varieties.name: Hollywood Hibiscus® 'Sunset Boulevard'
varieties.traits: Brilliant orange flowers with deep burgundy centers; blooms last 3 days; heat tolerant

varieties.name: 'Camdenii'
varieties.origin: 1800s, Camden Park near Sydney, imported by John Macarthur; single red form
varieties.traits: Historic Australian cultivar; single red hibiscus; robust growth; traditional form

varieties.name: 'Cameo Queen'
varieties.origin: Late 1940s import to Australia; large flowering variety that increased hibiscus interest
varieties.traits: Historic significance - one of first large-flowered varieties in Australia; robust grower

varieties.name: Hibiscus heterophyllus (Native Rosella) 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Eastern Australia, Lockhart River QLD to NSW
varieties.traits: Variable leaves (simple to lobed); prickly stems; fast-growing shrub 1.8-6m; traditional indigenous use
varieties.availability: Australian native plant nurseries, Queensland suppliers

varieties.name: Hibiscus tiliaceus (Cottonwood, Gh-gung) 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to coastal Queensland, tropical/subtropical species
varieties.traits: Spreading tree along mangroves/estuaries; heart-shaped leaves; salt-tolerant; traditional indigenous use
varieties.availability: Native plant nurseries, coastal restoration suppliers

varieties.name: Hibiscus splendens (Native Hollyhock) 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Northern NSW to Queensland; described by Colonial Botanist Charles Fraser as 'King of Australian plants'
varieties.traits: Spectacular flowering shrub; masses of flowers in spring; 2-4m tall

varieties.name: Hibiscus geranioides (Native Pink Hibiscus) 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to tropical QLD, NT, and WA; adaptable to cooler areas
varieties.traits: Small shrub to 1m; edible flowers and leaves; light frost tolerant
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, native plant specialists

varieties.name: Hibiscus divaricatus 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Queensland, Northern Territory, and Western Australia
varieties.traits: Native species adapted to northern Australia; variable form

varieties.name: Alyogyne huegelii 'West Coast Gem' 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Western Australia; cultivar of native species
varieties.traits: Purple-flowered form; dense growing; floriferous; wide climatic range; related to hibiscus; no prickly stems

varieties.name: Alyogyne huegelii 'Delightfully Double' 🌏
varieties.traits: Double-flowered form; compact growth; native hibiscus relative

varieties.name: Alyogyne huegelii 'Karana' 🌏
varieties.traits: Selected cultivar; reliable flowering; neat growth habit

varieties.name: 'Agnes Galt'
varieties.origin: Cultivar bred for cooler climates but adaptable
varieties.traits: Hardy variety; attractive pink flowers; reliable bloomer

varieties.name: 'Isobel Beard'
varieties.traits: Wonderful purple flowers; medium shrub; vigorous

varieties.name: 'White Kalakaua'
varieties.traits: Lovely double white flowers; vigorous Hawaiian variety

varieties.name: 'Catavki'
varieties.traits: Large blood-red flowers; classic Hawaiian variety; robust growth

varieties.name: 'Brilliant'
varieties.traits: Sturdy and floriferous; vigorous grower; classic landscape hibiscus

varieties.name: 'President'
varieties.traits: Traditional variety; robust growth; reliable performer

varieties.name: 'Hajdi Ho'
varieties.traits: Medium-sized single-petal blooms; soft pink with vibrant red center

varieties.name: 'Herm Geller'
varieties.traits: Earthy tones; rustic orange and muted pink; dramatic dark red center with golden stamens

varieties.name: 'Lemon Squash'
varieties.traits: Large bright yellow blooms with orange center; up to 18cm across

varieties.name: 'Leonie Witten'
varieties.traits: Captivating large vibrant pink flowers; modern hybrid

varieties.name: 'Rae Denise'
varieties.traits: Rich tapestry of autumnal shades; bronze, burnt orange, and muted gold petals

varieties.name: Hibiscus rosa-sinensis 'Single Red' 🌏
varieties.origin: Traditional form cultivated in India for centuries; known as 'Gudhal' (Hindi), 'Chemparati' (Malayalam)
varieties.traits: Classic single red form; vigorous shrub 2-3m; most common in Indian gardens; cultural and religious significance

varieties.name: Hibiscus rosa-sinensis 'Double Red' (Gudhal) 🌏
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian cultivar; known as 'Gudhal Flower' (Red Double)
varieties.traits: Double-flowered form; preferred for herbal and medicinal use; vibrant dark red blooms
varieties.availability: Widely available in tropical monsoon climates; Nurserylive, Mahindra Nursery, local growers

varieties.name: Hibiscus rosa-sinensis 'Shoeblack Plant' 🌏
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian/Indonesian variety; common name from historical use
varieties.traits: Dark red flowers used traditionally to shine shoes in tropics; hence 'shoeblack plant'

varieties.name: Hibiscus mutabilis (Cotton Rose)
varieties.traits: Unique color-changing flowers; broad green foliage with coarse texture; soft hairy leaves
varieties.availability: Nurserylive, Mahindra Nursery, Indian garden centers

varieties.name: 'Black Dragon'
varieties.origin: Modern hybrid; American Hibiscus Society 'Hibiscus of the Year'
varieties.traits: Deep burgundy (not truly black); award-winning variety; grows 3-6 feet tall

varieties.name: 'Blue Chiffon' (Hibiscus syriacus)
varieties.origin: Modern cultivar; Rose of Sharon variety
varieties.traits: Blue-flowering hibiscus; double-row petals create frilly appearance; heat and drought tolerant

varieties.name: 'Chasing Rainbows'
varieties.traits: Unique multi-colored variety; peach-pink with purple blending; buff, lavender, blue falls

varieties.name: 'Cherry Cheesecake'
varieties.traits: Creamy-white with magenta stripes; dark red center; thick ruffled petals; wind-resistant

varieties.name: 'Cosmic Dancer'
varieties.traits: Large single flowers; orange with red accents; exotic form; center colored orange, lavender, gold, maroon

varieties.name: 'Delta Dawn'
varieties.traits: Rare captivating variety; large 7-inch blooms

varieties.name: 'Donna Lynn'
varieties.traits: Large 8-10 inch flowers; vibrant and unique

varieties.name: 'Electric Plum'
varieties.traits: Unique gothic vibe; wide flaring petals; distinct dark coloration

varieties.name: 'Expect Miracles'
varieties.traits: Radiant variety; vibrant tropical vibe; rich orange shade often mistaken for red or pink

varieties.name: 'Galaxy' (Hibiscus moscheutos)
varieties.traits: Mesmerizing vibrant flowers; blooms grow approximately 6 inches across; summer perennial

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa var. sabdariffa 'Indian Red' 🌏 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety; extensively cultivated in India
varieties.traits: Red-variegated edible calyx; traditional heirloom; day-length sensitive; flowers late September-October; fruit matures November-December

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa var. sabdariffa 'African Red' (Zobo) 🏆
varieties.origin: West African variety; traditionally cultivated across Africa
varieties.traits: Red edible calyx; takes longer to flower; day-length sensitive; used for beverages 'zobo'
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, specialty heirloom seed companies

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa var. sabdariffa 'Indian Green' 🌏
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety; actually Hibiscus cannabinus (kenaf)
varieties.traits: Green edible calyx; flowers readily after 12-14 weeks; leaves primary use in India
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates vegetable seed suppliers, agricultural stores

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa var. sabdariffa 'Rouge' 🏆
varieties.origin: Selected heirloom variety; USDA trial variety
varieties.traits: Large loose calyces; easy to peel; beautiful red color; open-pollinated; plant May-July, harvest Thanksgiving-New Year
varieties.availability: Hawaii Seed Growers Network, specialty heirloom suppliers

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa 'St. Kitts Roselle' 🏆
varieties.traits: Produces larger calyxes than other varieties; ideal for southern climates; 6-7' tall; up to 12 lbs calyces per plant
varieties.availability: HOSS Tools, Foraged, heirloom seed companies

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa 'Jamaica Sorrel' 🌏 🏆
varieties.origin: Caribbean/West Indian traditional variety; known as 'sorrel' in Jamaica, 'flor de jamaica' in Mexico
varieties.traits: Traditional heirloom; tart cranberry-like flavor; open-pollinated; Christmas drink in Caribbean

varieties.name: Hibiscus sabdariffa var. altissima 'Fiber Type' 🌏
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety; cultivated for jute-like fiber
varieties.traits: Erect sparsely-branched annual to 16 ft (4.8m); green or red stems; cultivated in India for fiber; non-fleshy calyces not for food
varieties.availability: Agricultural fiber crop suppliers in India

varieties.name: 'Anne Arundel'
varieties.traits: Compact vigorous shrub; flowers up to 9 inches diameter; clear pink

varieties.name: 'Fiji Island'
varieties.traits: Traditional Pacific cultivar; tropical origins; robust growth

varieties.name: 'Mahoe'
varieties.traits: Popular tropical variety; robust tropical growth

varieties.name: 'Red Hot'
varieties.traits: Vibrant red flowers; heat-loving; tropical favorite

varieties.name: 'Chinese Lantern'
varieties.traits: Asian tropical variety; distinctive form

varieties.name: 'Mango Liqueur'
varieties.origin: Modern hybrid (cross: 'Muffin Man' x 'Crème de Cacao')
varieties.traits: Beautiful orange-gold ruffled flowers; vigorous bush 10-12 feet; used in decorations and weddings

varieties.name: 'Tricolor' (Variegated)
varieties.traits: Variegated leaves in pink, white, green; deep red flowers with yellow anthers; made by grafting

varieties.name: 'Luna Red' (Hibiscus moscheutos)
varieties.traits: Compact bushy plant; five-petaled blossoms; dies back in winter, returns summer; bright green foliage

varieties.name: Hibiscus tiliaceus 'Sea Hibiscus' 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Pacific and Indian Ocean shores; widespread in coastal tropics
varieties.traits: Coastal tree/shrub; salt-tolerant; poor soil tolerant; strong wind resistant; traditional use for boats

varieties.name: Hibiscus acetosella 'False Roselle'
varieties.traits: Red/bronze five-lobed leaves; red stems to 8 ft; young leaves edible; acid flavor

varieties.name: Abelmoschus moschatus subsp. tuberosus 'Musk Mallow' 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to tropical Queensland and Northern Territory; hibiscus relative
varieties.traits: Brilliant red flowers; perennial; thrives in tropical areas; hibiscus-like flowers
